articles.title: Trump says he will have the ‘honor’ of ‘taking Cuba’
articles.description:
Trump’s words came amid a nationwide blackout and as a top Cuban official said his country would move to open the economy to foreign investors.

The post Trump says he will have the ‘honor’ of ‘taking Cuba’ appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Trump expressed intentions to engage with Cuba amid economic changes on March 16, 2026.
Cuban officials plan to open the economy to foreign investors, impacting local businesses.
Boston residents may see increased trade opportunities and cultural exchanges with Cuba.

articles.title: Kennedy Center votes to shut down operations for 2 years and names a new president
articles.description:
“We’re going to ensure it remains the finest performing arts facility of its kind anywhere in the world," Trump told reporters at the White House before the board met Monday.

The post Kennedy Center votes to shut down operations for 2 years and names a new president appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kennedy Center will cease operations for two years starting immediately.
New president appointed to lead the center during its closure.
Local arts community may face challenges due to the center's temporary shutdown.

articles.title: A Utah woman who wrote a book on grief after husband’s death found guilty of murdering him
articles.description:
Jurors on Monday also found Kouri Richins guilty of fraudulently claiming insurance benefits after the death of Eric Richins in March 2022 at their home outside the ski town of Park City.

The post A Utah woman who wrote a book on grief after husband’s death found guilty of murdering him appeared first on Boston.com.
summary: Kouri Richins found guilty of murdering husband Eric Richins in March 2022.
Jurors also convicted her of fraudulently claiming insurance benefits.
This case raises concerns about domestic violence awareness in Boston communities.
